title: Mzansibet Comboboost: Up to 15% Boost on Man Utd, Palace and Liverpool This Weekend
author: Thabo Mokoena
excerpt: Mzansibet is offering a 15% Comboboost on combo bets featuring Man Utd vs Aston Villa, Crystal Palace vs Leeds, and Liverpool vs Spurs this weekend. Here is how to use it.
body:
Mzansibet has dropped a weekend Comboboost for EPL fans. If you combine Man Utd, Crystal Palace, and Liverpool to win into a combo bet, you can boost your payout by up to 15%.

Which matches are included?

The boost applies to three EPL fixtures this weekend:

  • Manchester United vs Aston Villa
  • Crystal Palace vs Leeds United
  • Liverpool FC vs Tottenham Hotspur

You need to include all three matches in a combo bet with 3 or more selections to activate the boost. All markets are eligible - so you can use match result, BTTS, over/under, or any other market on these fixtures.

How the Comboboost works

The Comboboost adds up to 15% on top of your normal combo winnings. It applies automatically when you build a qualifying bet slip - no promo code needed. The boost amount is calculated based on the number of selections and the odds of each leg.

For a 3-leg combo using these matches, you get a 1.15x multiplier on your winnings. The more legs you add, the higher the boost potential. Both the original winnings and the Comboboost amount are paid out as a single transaction.

Key terms

  • Available for combo bets with 3 or more events
  • Each event must have odds of 1.00 or higher
  • Pre-match bets only (no live/in-play)
  • Offer valid from 13 March 15:00 to 15 March 20:30
  • Limited to 1 use per customer
  • The boost is applied automatically - you cannot change the bonus amount
  • Cannot be combined with other promotions

Is it worth it?

A 15% boost on a combo bet is solid value, especially since it applies across all markets on these fixtures. If you were planning to build a weekend accumulator around EPL games anyway, this is free extra value on top.

Man Utd at home, Palace at home, and Liverpool at home are three results many punters would back regardless. The Comboboost just sweetens the deal.

For the full picture on this bookmaker, check out our Mzansibet review or grab the Mzansibet promo code if you are signing up fresh.

title: World Sports Betting Monday Wheel Extended Until May 2026 - Over R10 Million Given Away
author: Sipho Nkosi
excerpt: World Sports Betting has extended the Monday Wheel promotion until 31 May 2026 after giving away over R10 million in World Coins. Log in every Monday for a free spin, plus a shot at the R50,000 cash prize draw.
body:
World Sports Betting has extended its popular Monday Wheel promotion until 31 May 2026 after the original run proved so successful that over R10 million in World Coins has already been given away.

How it works

Every Monday, log in to your World Sports Betting account and spin the wheel for free. Each spin awards a random prize in World Coins (free bets), and every spin also enters you into the R50,000 cash prize draw at the end of the promotion.

There is no catch and no deposit required to spin. It is one free spin per account per week, available every Monday during the promotional period.

How to claim your prize

  1. Log in every Monday
  2. Spin the wheel
  3. Click the claim button that appears after spinning
  4. Free bets are credited to your World Coins balance (may take up to 30 minutes)

Important: you must click the claim pop-up button on the Monday you spin. If you miss it, the prize is forfeited.

R50,000 cash prize draw

On top of the weekly free bets, every spin enters you into a grand prize draw for R50,000 in cash. The draw takes place on 2 June 2026, and the winner will be contacted within 48 hours. The more Mondays you spin, the more entries you accumulate.

Key terms

  • Promotion runs from 24 November 2025 to 31 May 2026
  • One free spin per account per Monday
  • FICA-verified accounts only
  • Free bet turnover: 5x on sports, 30x on casino
  • R50,000 cash draw on 2 June 2026
  • Account must be active and in good standing

Is it worth it?

It is a free spin with zero cost to you, so the answer is straightforward. Even if your prize is a small free bet, it is still free value on top of your normal betting activity. The R50,000 cash draw is the real incentive to log in consistently every Monday.

If you do not already have an account, check out our World Sports Betting review or grab the WSB promo code for the welcome bonus on sign-up.

title: betXchange Red Baron Lucky Draw: Win a Share of R200,000 in Shopping Vouchers
author: Lerato Dlamini
excerpt: betXchange and Evolution are giving away R200,000 in Pick n Pay vouchers through the Red Baron crash game. Bet R2 or more to earn entries into the lucky draw running until 29 March 2026.
body:
betXchange has teamed up with Evolution to launch the Red Baron Lucky Draw Takeoff promotion, putting R200,000 in Pick n Pay shopping vouchers up for grabs. The promotion runs from 1 March to 29 March 2026.

How it works

Red Baron is a crash game from Evolution where a vintage biplane takes off and the multiplier climbs. Cash out before the plane disappears to lock in your win. Every bet of R2 or more on Red Baron earns you an entry into the lucky draw - and there is no limit to the number of entries you can submit.

Tickets are allocated regardless of the outcome the game round, so every qualifying bet counts. Only real money bets qualify - bonus funds do not count.

Prize breakdown

  • 200 winners will be randomly selected
  • Each winner receives a R1,000 Pick n Pay shopping voucher
  • Total prize pool: R200,000

Key dates

  • Promotion period: 1 March - 29 March 2026
  • Audited draw: 30 March 2026
  • Winner lists sent to operators: 31 March 2026
  • Vouchers distributed: 1 April 2026

The draw will be audited by an independent accounting firm, and all qualifying tickets across opted-in operators will be placed into a virtual vault from which the 200 winners are randomly selected.

How to enter

To participate, you need to:

  • Have a betXchange account
  • Opt in to the promotion via the tick box (required for POPIA compliance)
  • Place real money bets of R2 or more on Red Baron

The more you play, the more entries you accumulate. At R2 per qualifying bet, it is one of the lowest entry barriers for a promotion of this size in South Africa.

Is it worth it?

R200,000 split across 200 winners means everyone who wins gets a R1,000 Pick n Pay voucher. The odds depend on total entries across all participating operators, but with a R2 minimum bet on a game you might be playing anyway, there is no real downside to opting in.

If you enjoy crash games, Red Baron is worth trying. For more on how crash games work, read our Aviator and Crash Games guide. Or check out the betXchange review and grab the betXchange promo code before signing up.

title: betXchange Jackpot Challenge: R2 Million and 2 Cars Up for Grabs This EPL Season
author: Sipho Nkosi
excerpt: betXchange is running a season-long Jackpot Challenge tied to the English Premier League. Predict weekly match winners for a shot at a growing cash pot, plus two cars at the end of the season.
body:
betXchange has launched its biggest promotion of the year. The 25/26 Jackpot Challenge runs for the entire 2025-2026 English Premier League season, with R2 million in prize money and two cars on the line.

How the Jackpot Challenge works

Each EPL game week, betXchange adds R31,000 to the prize pot. Customers predict the winners of that week's fixtures. Get them all right and you take home the pot. If nobody nails it, the money rolls over to the next week - meaning the jackpot can grow significantly as the season progresses.

If more than one person predicts correctly, the pot is split equally. Two winners on a R31,000 week means R15,500 each.

Two cars up for grabs

On top of the weekly cash prize, every entry earns you a ticket in the end-of-season car draw. The more weeks you enter, the more chances you have to win one of the two cars. It is a genuine incentive to keep entering throughout the season rather than dipping in for one week and forgetting about it.

How to enter

To qualify for each week's Jackpot Challenge, you need to place a qualifying bet at any betXchange branch or on www.betxchange.co.za. The qualifying bet must meet these conditions:

  • It must be placed on any soccer league
  • It must be a multiple bet (accumulator) with at least 2 legs
  • All legs must be soccer bets
  • Minimum stake of R10
  • The bet must be placed during the open prediction window for that game week

No special promo code is needed. Just place a qualifying bet and you are automatically entered for that week's prediction challenge.

Key terms to know

A few important details from the T&Cs:

  • The promotion is only open to customers who bet at listed betXchange branches or on betxchange.co.za
  • The challenge runs for the full 2025-2026 EPL season
  • If no one predicts correctly, the R31,000 carries over to the following week
  • Car draw entries accumulate - more weeks entered means more chances

Is it worth entering?

At R10 minimum stake on a soccer accumulator you were probably going to place anyway, the barrier to entry is very low. The rollover mechanic means the pot can reach serious numbers if a few weeks pass without a winner. And the car draw is a genuine bonus for consistent players.

If you are already betting on EPL football, there is no reason not to opt in. Check out our full betXchange review for the full picture on this operator, or grab the latest betXchange promo code before signing up.

title: Apexbets Drops a Midweek Reload Bonus Worth Up to 200%
author: Lerato Dlamini
excerpt: Apexbets is offering existing customers a midweek reload bonus of up to 200% on deposits. Here is how it works and whether it is worth your time.
body:
Apexbets has rolled out a Midweek Reload Bonus that gives existing customers up to 200% extra on their deposits. It is a solid play for punters who like to top up during the week - but as always, the devil is in the T&Cs.

What is on offer?

The Midweek Reload Bonus is a tiered deposit match available every Wednesday and Thursday. Depending on how much you deposit, you unlock a different match percentage:

  • Deposit R50 - R199: 50% bonus match
  • Deposit R200 - R499: 100% bonus match
  • Deposit R500+: 200% bonus match

So if you drop R500 into your account on a Wednesday, you walk away with R1,500 in total - your original deposit plus R1,000 in bonus funds. Not bad for a midweek punt.

How to claim it

There is no promo code needed for this one. The bonus is applied automatically when you make a qualifying deposit on Wednesday or Thursday. You do need to be an existing Apexbets customer with a verified account.

The fine print

Before you get too excited, here are the key terms you need to know:

  • Minimum qualifying deposit is R50
  • The bonus must be wagered 5x before withdrawal
  • Only bets with odds of 1.80 or higher count towards the rollover
  • The bonus expires 7 days after being credited
  • One bonus per customer per promotional period
  • Available on Wednesdays and Thursdays only

The 5x wagering requirement at 1.80 odds is actually pretty reasonable by SA standards. Some operators ask for 8x or 10x, so Apexbets is being fair here. The 7-day expiry window gives you enough room to work through the rollover without rushing.

Is it worth it?

For regular punters who were going to deposit anyway, this is free value. The 200% tier at R500+ is particularly generous - you are tripling your bankroll for the week. Even the 50% tier at R50 minimum is accessible for casual bettors who just want a little extra for the midweek fixtures.

The main thing to watch is the wagering requirement. Make sure you are placing bets you would normally place at odds you are comfortable with. Do not chase bad bets just to clear the rollover.

For the full rundown on this operator, read our Apexbets review, or compare their current offers on our Apexbets promo code page.

title: Lucky Fish Launches Mystery Parcel Promo with R450,000 in Prizes
author: Lerato Dlamini
excerpt: Lucky Fish and Playtech put R450,000 on the table with the Mystery Parcel promo. Twice a week, spin selected slots for a shot at random cash prizes and bonus spins.
body:
Lucky Fish has gone big with its latest casino push. The Mystery Parcel promotion, powered by Playtech, puts R450,000 in prizes up for grabs - and the entry bar is just R2 a spin.

The deal

Bet R2 or more on qualifying Playtech slots during the promo windows and you are automatically entered. No opt-in forms, no codes. The promotion runs on a set timetable:

  • Tuesdays: 18:00 to 00:00
  • Saturdays: 12:00 to 18:00

Each window is its own draw, so you get two bites at the cherry every week.

What is up for grabs?

Mystery Parcels drop randomly during the promo windows. You could land cash payouts or bonus spins credited straight to your account. There is no leaderboard and no head-to-head competition - it is purely random, which keeps things fair for smaller bankrolls.

Worth your time?

R450,000 is a chunky prize pool by SA standards, especially for a recurring promo. Playtech events tend to be well-run and the twice-weekly schedule gives regulars plenty of chances. If you are already spinning Playtech titles at Lucky Fish, you are essentially getting free shots at extra value on top of your normal play.

The R2 minimum keeps it accessible - you do not need deep pockets to get involved. That said, the usual responsible gambling rules apply. Set your limits before you chase mystery parcels.

For the full picture on this operator, check out our Lucky Fish review or grab the latest Lucky Fish promo code. If you prefer other casino-focused platforms, PlayTsogo and SaffaLuck also run regular slot promotions.

title: Cape Town's Wale Street Has a New Resident, and It's Making the Bookies Sweat
author: Sipho Nkosi
excerpt: Easybet opens its first physical presence on Wale Street in Cape Town CBD, signalling a push into the Western Cape market.
body:
Easybet has planted its flag in the Mother City. The operator opened a branded shopfront on Wale Street in the Cape Town CBD - its first physical location in the Western Cape and a clear statement about where it sees growth.

Why it matters

Most SA betting brands operate purely online. Opening a physical space is expensive, so when a brand does it, they are betting (literally) on that market. Wale Street sits in the heart of the city centre, close to the taxi ranks and main transport hubs where foot traffic is high.

For punters in Cape Town, it means easier access to cash deposits and face-to-face support - two things that online-only operators struggle with. A lot of bettors in SA still prefer handing over cash to tapping a card number into an app.

The bigger picture

Easybet is not the first to try this. Hollywoodbets has built a massive retail network across KZN and Gauteng, and Betway has invested heavily in brand visibility at malls and sports venues. But the Western Cape has been underserved by retail betting shops compared to Joburg or Durban.

Whether this is a one-off or the start of a broader rollout remains to be seen. But for now, Cape Town punters have a new option on their doorstep.

Read our full Easybet review to see how they stack up, or compare them with Hollywoodbets and Betway.

title: Zarbet Launches Industry-First Cashback Loyalty Programme for SA Punters
excerpt: Zarbet rolls out a cashback loyalty programme that returns a percentage of net losses weekly. Here is how it works and who it suits.
body:
Zarbet has launched a cashback loyalty programme that gives players a percentage of their net weekly losses back. It is not a flashy one-time bonus - it is a recurring perk aimed at keeping regular punters coming back.

How the cashback works

The structure is straightforward. At the end of each week, Zarbet calculates your net losses across qualifying bets and credits a percentage back to your account. The exact rate depends on your activity level, with higher-volume players earning a bigger slice.

Credits are paid as bonus funds with a turnover requirement before withdrawal. The T&Cs are not unusual by industry standards, but read them carefully before assuming you can cash out immediately.

Is it actually good?

Cashback programmes are the most honest type of loyalty reward in betting. Unlike deposit matches that require you to put in new money, cashback softens the blow on money you have already lost. For regular bettors, it adds up over time.

Hollywoodbets runs a similar Top Up loyalty scheme, and Betway offers VIP cashback tiers for high-volume players. Zarbet's version seems pitched at the middle ground - everyday punters who bet consistently but are not necessarily high rollers.

Our view

If you are already a Zarbet regular, activate the programme. Free money on losses is never a bad thing. If you are shopping around, this tips the scales slightly in Zarbet's favour for long-term value.

Check out our full Zarbet review or grab the Zarbet promo code to get started.
author: Sipho Nkosi

title: FICA Verification Guide: Everything SA Bettors Need to Know
author: Sipho Nkosi
excerpt: FICA verification trips up thousands of SA bettors every month. Here is what you actually need, how long it takes, and how to avoid delays.
body:
If you have ever tried to withdraw from a South African betting site and been hit with a FICA request, you are not alone. Verification delays are the single biggest complaint from SA bettors - and most of the frustration comes from not knowing what to expect.

What FICA actually requires

Under South Africa's Financial Intelligence Centre Act, every betting operator must verify your identity before processing withdrawals above certain thresholds. In practice, most sites ask for documents upfront or after your first withdrawal request.

You will typically need:

  • Proof of identity: SA ID book, smart ID card, or valid passport
  • Proof of address: Utility bill or bank statement less than 3 months old
  • Proof of banking: Bank statement showing your name and account number

Where it goes wrong

The most common issues are blurry photos, expired documents, and mismatched names. If the name on your betting account does not match your ID exactly, you will hit a wall. Some operators also require a selfie holding your ID - it varies.

Processing times differ wildly between operators. Hollywoodbets and Betway generally verify within 24-48 hours. Smaller platforms can take up to a week, especially over weekends.

How to speed things up

Submit your documents the day you register - do not wait for your first withdrawal. Use a well-lit photo, make sure all four corners of the document are visible, and double-check that nothing is cut off. The cleaner your submission, the faster the turnaround.

For a detailed walkthrough, read our full FICA verification guide. If you are comparing operators on verification speed, our Hollywoodbets and Betway reviews cover their respective processes.

title: Betway South Africa Adds 50 New Cricket Markets Ahead of T20 Season
excerpt: Betway SA expands its cricket markets with ball-by-ball betting, player props, and live streaming for SA domestic and international fixtures.
body:
Betway has expanded its cricket offering for South African punters, adding ball-by-ball markets, player performance props, and live streaming across domestic and international fixtures. It is a serious upgrade for cricket bettors in Mzansi.

What has changed

The expanded markets cover CSA T20 Challenge, SA20, and all Proteas internationals. You can now bet on individual deliveries, top batsman per over, method of dismissal, and run totals per partnership - the kind of granular markets that were previously only available on offshore platforms.

Live streaming is the real headline here. Being able to watch the match and bet in-play on the same platform removes the need to juggle between apps. Betway's stream quality has been solid in our testing, with minimal delay.

How it compares

10Bet and Hollywoodbets both offer cricket markets, but neither matches the depth Betway is now providing. Hollywoodbets covers the basics - match winner, top batsman, total runs - but lacks the ball-by-ball granularity. 10Bet has better pre-match variety but its live betting interface for cricket still needs work.

Who benefits

If you are a cricket punter who bets in-play, this is a significant improvement. The combination of deep markets and live streaming on one platform is hard to beat in the SA market right now.

Read our full Betway review for the complete breakdown, or compare with 10Bet and Hollywoodbets.
author: Thabo Mokoena

title: Easybet Increases Welcome Bonus to R5,000 Deposit Match for March
excerpt: Easybet is running a R5,000 deposit match for March. We break down the wagering requirements, qualifying criteria, and whether the value stacks up.
body:
Easybet is offering a 100% deposit match up to R5,000 for new sign-ups this March. On paper, it is one of the biggest welcome offers in the SA market right now. But the devil is in the details.

The offer

Register a new account, deposit between R50 and R5,000, and Easybet matches it rand-for-rand as bonus funds. The bonus comes with a 5x wagering requirement on odds of 1.80 or higher before you can withdraw.

Is the wagering fair?

A 5x turnover at 1.80+ odds is actually reasonable by SA standards. Supabets typically asks for 3x but caps its welcome bonus lower. Gbets runs similar deposit matches but with tighter odds restrictions. Zarbet and Betway sit somewhere in the middle.

The key thing to watch is the expiry. Most deposit match bonuses at SA bookmakers expire within 7-14 days, so you need to turn them over quickly. Easybet gives you 30 days, which is more generous than most.

Our take

R5,000 is a headline number, but only deposit what you can afford to lose. If you were going to sign up with Easybet anyway, this sweetens the deal nicely. If you are just bonus hunting, factor in the wagering before you commit.

Grab the Easybet promo code to activate the offer, and read our full Easybet review for the wider picture.
author: Sipho Nkosi

title: Supabets Adds Live Streaming for 10 Additional Sports
excerpt: Supabets adds live streaming across 10 new sports including basketball, tennis, and table tennis. Here is what is actually watchable.
body:
Supabets has rolled out live streaming for 10 additional sports on its platform. The expansion covers basketball, tennis, table tennis, volleyball, handball, futsal, badminton, beach volleyball, ice hockey, and snooker.

What you can actually watch

Coverage varies by sport. Basketball and tennis get the most consistent streams, with major leagues and ATP/WTA events available throughout the week. Table tennis and futsal streams are more hit-and-miss - you will find Eastern European leagues and lower-tier fixtures rather than headline events.

Stream quality is decent on wifi but can buffer on mobile data, especially 3G connections. There is no dedicated streaming tab yet, so you need to go to the live event and look for the play icon.

How it stacks up

Betway remains the benchmark for live streaming in SA, with smoother playback and a wider range of Tier 1 events. Hollywoodbets has also expanded its streaming recently, though it leans heavily on horse racing and football.

Supabets' advantage is the variety. If you like betting on niche sports in-play, having the stream and the markets on one screen removes friction.

Worth checking out?

If you are already a Supabets user, absolutely - it costs nothing and it enhances your live betting. If streaming quality is your top priority, Betway still has the edge.

Read our full Supabets review for the complete feature breakdown.
author: Thabo Mokoena

title: Hollywoodbets Spina Zonke Pays Out R2.3 Million Jackpot to Durban Punter
excerpt: Another Spina Zonke jackpot hits at Hollywoodbets, with a KZN player landing a six-figure payout on the popular slot platform.
body:
A KwaZulu-Natal player has hit a six-figure jackpot on Hollywoodbets' Spina Zonke slot platform. The win, confirmed by the operator this week, adds to a growing list of big payouts from what has become SA's most popular online slots product.

Spina Zonke keeps delivering

Spina Zonke ("Spin All" in Zulu) has become a phenomenon in the SA online gambling space. The platform hosts a range of slot titles with progressive jackpots that pool across all players. When they pop, they can be life-changing - and they have been hitting with increasing frequency as the player base grows.

This latest winner reportedly staked a modest amount before triggering the jackpot on a progressive title. Hollywoodbets has not disclosed the exact game or the full amount, but sources indicate it was well into six figures.

Can you realistically win?

The honest answer: the odds are long, as with any progressive jackpot. But the fact that winners keep emerging shows the prize pools are real and the games do pay out. The minimum stake to qualify for jackpots is low, which is part of the appeal.

If slots are your thing, Betway and YesPlay also offer progressive jackpot games, and Jackpot City is purpose-built for casino players. But Spina Zonke's brand recognition in SA is hard to beat.

Check out our Hollywoodbets review for a full breakdown of their casino offering, or browse Hollywoodbets promo codes for the latest bonus.
author: Lerato Dlamini

title: Gbets Signs Sponsorship Deal with Sharks Rugby
excerpt: Gbets signs a sponsorship deal with the Sharks rugby franchise, boosting its brand visibility in KZN and signalling a push into rugby betting markets.
body:
Gbets has signed a sponsorship partnership with the Sharks, one of South Africa's biggest rugby franchises. The deal puts the Gbets brand on match-day assets at Hollywoodbets Kings Park in Durban and across the Sharks' digital channels.

What the deal covers

Gbets branding will appear on LED boards, interview backdrops, and in-stadium signage during all Sharks home fixtures in the URC and Currie Cup. The partnership also includes digital activations and promotions linked to Sharks match days.

Financial terms have not been disclosed, but sports sponsorships at this level typically run into millions of rands per season.

The strategy behind it

KZN is the heartland of SA rugby and a massive betting market. By aligning with the Sharks, Gbets gets its name in front of a passionate, loyal fanbase that overlaps heavily with its target demographic. It is the same playbook Hollywoodbets used when it secured naming rights to Kings Park - and that worked spectacularly.

Betway has focused on football sponsorships (the PSL deal), while World Sports Betting and Sportingbet have spread their marketing across multiple codes. Gbets' rugby-first approach is a clear bet on the sport's cultural weight in KZN.

What it means for punters

Expect Sharks-linked promotions, enhanced rugby odds on match days, and possibly free-to-play prediction games tied to fixtures. Gbets has done this before with cricket, so the template exists.

Read our full Gbets review for the complete platform breakdown.
author: Thabo Mokoena

title: 10Bet South Africa Launches Redesigned Mobile App with Faster Cash Out
excerpt: 10Bet SA launches a rebuilt mobile app with faster load times, a cleaner bet slip, and biometric login. We tested it.
body:
10Bet has released a rebuilt mobile app for South African users. The update brings a new interface, faster load times, and biometric login - addressing the main complaints users had with the old version.

What is new

The app has been rebuilt from the ground up rather than patched. Key changes include:

  • Faster navigation: Page loads are noticeably quicker, especially in the live betting section
  • Cleaner bet slip: The multi-bet builder is more intuitive, with running totals visible at all times
  • Biometric login: Fingerprint and face unlock for quicker access
  • Improved cash-out: The partial cash-out feature now works in real time without the lag that plagued the old version

How it compares

Betway's app is still the smoothest in the SA market, but the gap has narrowed. Easybet's app is fast but lighter on features. 10Bet's new version sits comfortably in the top tier now, especially for bettors who value live betting and quick cash-outs.

Our verdict

If you dropped 10Bet because of app issues, it deserves a second look. The improvement is significant. Available on both Android (APK download) and iOS (App Store).

Full details in our 10Bet review, or grab the 10Bet promo code for new sign-ups.
author: Sipho Nkosi

title: YesPlay Enters Crash Games Market with Aviator and Three New Titles
excerpt: YesPlay adds Aviator and a suite of crash games to its casino section. Fast-paced, high-risk, and extremely popular with SA players.
body:
YesPlay has added Spribe's Aviator and a range of crash-style games to its platform. The titles join an already solid casino section and tap into what has become one of the fastest-growing game categories in South Africa.

What is available

The headline addition is Aviator, the multiplier game where you cash out before the plane flies away. It has become a cultural phenomenon in SA betting circles - social media is full of screenshots from players who caught a 50x or 100x multiplier (and plenty who did not).

Alongside Aviator, YesPlay has added Mines, Plinko, Hi-Lo, and several other crash variants. These are quick-fire games that appeal to players who want instant results rather than waiting for a football match to finish.

Are they fair?

Spribe's games use provably fair technology, meaning each round can be independently verified. This is a step above standard RNG games where you have to trust the operator's word. Betway and Hollywoodbets also offer Aviator through the same Spribe integration, so the game mechanics are identical across platforms.

Word of caution

Crash games are addictive by design. The rounds are fast, the highs are intense, and it is very easy to chase losses. Set strict session limits before you play. The house edge on Aviator sits around 3%, which is fair for a casino game, but the speed of play means you can burn through a bankroll quickly.

Check our YesPlay review for the full casino breakdown, or see how Betway and Hollywoodbets compare for Aviator players.
author: Lerato Dlamini

title: Mzansibet Now Offers Instant Payouts via Ozow
excerpt: Mzansibet integrates Ozow for instant EFT payouts. We tested withdrawal times and compared them across SA operators.
body:
Mzansibet now supports Ozow instant EFT for withdrawals, cutting payout times from the usual 24-48 hours down to minutes. For a mid-tier SA operator, that is a significant upgrade.

How fast is it really?

In our testing, an Ozow withdrawal from Mzansibet hit the bank account in under 15 minutes. That was on a weekday afternoon with FICA already completed. First-time withdrawals will still be slower due to verification, but repeat cashouts should be near-instant.

Why this matters

Withdrawal speed is one of the biggest factors SA bettors care about - and one of the most common complaints. Operators that still rely on manual EFT processing can take 1-3 business days. Ozow bypasses this by connecting directly to your bank via instant EFT.

Betway and Hollywoodbets already support Ozow, so Mzansibet is catching up rather than leading. But for existing Mzansibet users, this removes a genuine pain point. Easybet also added Ozow recently, and the trend is clearly moving towards instant payouts as the baseline expectation.

Any downsides?

Ozow works with most major SA banks (FNB, Standard Bank, Nedbank, Absa, Capitec) but not all. If your bank is not supported, you are still stuck with standard EFT. There are also daily withdrawal limits that vary by operator.

Read our full Mzansibet review for the complete payment breakdown, or browse our payment methods guide for a cross-platform comparison.
author: Naledi Khumalo

title: National Gambling Board Launches R10 Million Responsible Betting Campaign
excerpt: The National Gambling Board launches a new responsible betting campaign targeting young adults. Here is what operators are expected to do.
body:
The National Gambling Board (NGB) has launched a public awareness campaign on responsible betting, with a specific focus on 18-25 year olds. The campaign includes TV spots, social media content, and new guidelines for how operators should promote responsible gambling tools.

What the campaign covers

The NGB's push targets three areas: deposit limit awareness, self-exclusion options, and the normalisation of betting in youth culture. South Africa has seen a sharp rise in online betting among young adults, driven largely by Aviator, Spina Zonke, and the proliferation of betting apps.

The campaign materials encourage bettors to set deposit limits, take regular breaks, and use self-exclusion tools when gambling stops being fun.

What operators must do

Licensed operators are expected to display responsible gambling messaging more prominently on their platforms. Hollywoodbets, Betway, Supabets, and World Sports Betting already have responsible gambling sections, but the NGB wants these to be harder to miss - not buried in footer links.

Expect to see pop-up reminders about session duration, clearer deposit limit settings, and potentially mandatory cool-off periods after extended sessions.

Our position

This is a welcome move. The growth of online betting in SA has outpaced the regulatory framework, and younger players are particularly vulnerable. We encourage all readers to use the tools available - every reputable operator offers deposit limits, loss limits, and self-exclusion.

Read our responsible gambling guide and our support organisations page if you or someone you know needs help.
author: Thabo Mokoena

title: Pantherbet Launches Virtual Sports with 24/7 Betting Markets
excerpt: Pantherbet goes live with virtual sports covering football, horse racing, greyhounds, and motorsport. Quick results, 24/7 availability.
body:
Pantherbet has launched a virtual sports section featuring football, horse racing, greyhound racing, and motorsport. Events run around the clock with results every 2-3 minutes - no fixture lists, no off-seasons.

What is on offer

Virtual football is the standout, with simulated league matches using realistic odds and stats. Horse racing and greyhounds follow the standard virtual format - short races with fixed odds available right up to the off. Motorsport is the newest addition, offering head-to-head and podium finish markets.

Graphics are decent without being spectacular. The animations are functional rather than cinematic, but for bettors who care about odds rather than visuals, that is fine.

Who is this for?

Virtual sports fill the gap when there is nothing live to bet on. Late at night, midweek, during international breaks - there is always something running. They also suit bettors who want quick-fire action without waiting 90 minutes for a football result.

Betway has the most polished virtual sports offering in SA, while Gbets and Playabets also carry a solid selection. Pantherbet's entry to the space adds another option, though the range is still narrower than the bigger operators.

A word on virtual sports

The outcomes are generated by RNG (random number generators), not based on real-world form. Past results do not predict future ones. Treat them as casino-style entertainment with sports packaging, not as something you can analyse and beat through research.

Full details in our Pantherbet review.
author: Lerato Dlamini

title: World Sports Betting Launches Free-to-Play Football Predictor with R500,000 Prize Pool
excerpt: World Sports Betting launches a free-to-play predictor game with weekly cash prizes. No deposit required to enter.
body:
World Sports Betting has rolled out a free-to-play predictor game where registered users can win cash prizes without staking a cent. Pick the correct results from a weekly slate of fixtures and you are in the running.

How it works

Each week, WSB selects a set of fixtures across football, rugby, and cricket. You predict the outcomes - match winner, correct score, or over/under depending on the round - and submit your picks before kick-off. Get them all right and you share the prize pool.

Registration is free and there is no deposit requirement to play. You just need a verified WSB account.

Why operators do this

Free-to-play games are a customer acquisition tool. WSB gets you onto the platform, verified and familiar with the interface, in the hope you will eventually start betting with real money. It is a well-tested strategy - Hollywoodbets, Betway, and Sportingbet all run similar prediction games at various points during the season.

Is it worth playing?

If you are already a WSB user, there is zero downside - it costs nothing. If you are not, it is a risk-free way to try the platform. The prizes are modest compared to actual betting payouts, but free money is free money.

The quality of the experience depends on how well WSB maintains the game. Some operators launch these and quietly let them die; others keep the prize pools fresh and the fixture selection interesting.

Read our full World Sports Betting review for the wider platform assessment.
author: Sipho Nkosi

title: Betway South Africa Launches New Aviator Game Mode with R500 Free Play
excerpt: Betway SA upgrades its Aviator experience with a new interface, auto cash-out tweaks, and higher max multipliers.
author: Thabo Mokoena
body:
Betway has updated its Aviator game with a refreshed interface, improved auto cash-out settings, and higher maximum multipliers. The changes make an already popular game smoother and potentially more rewarding.

What changed

The new interface is cleaner, with a bigger game area and less clutter around the edges. The live bet feed showing other players' stakes and cash-outs is still there but has been redesigned to be less distracting. Auto cash-out now allows more precise settings - you can set it to 1.47x or 2.13x rather than being locked to round numbers.

The maximum multiplier has reportedly been raised, though Betway has not published the exact ceiling. In the old version, rounds occasionally hit 100x+; the new version should allow even higher peaks.

Does it change the odds?

No. The underlying mathematics remain the same. Aviator's house edge sits around 3% regardless of the interface. Higher max multipliers do not mean higher average payouts - they just mean the rare monster rounds can go further.

Aviator's hold on SA

Aviator has become the most talked-about game in South African online gambling. The combination of simplicity, speed, and social proof (watching others cash out in real time) is hugely compelling. But it is also the game most associated with problem gambling complaints, particularly among younger players.

Play responsibly. Set a session budget and stick to it. Check out our Betway review for the full platform breakdown, or compare Aviator offerings at YesPlay and Hollywoodbets.

title: Easybet Extends 150% Match Bonus Through March 2026
excerpt: Easybet bumps its deposit match to 150% for a limited March promotion. We look at the terms and whether the extra percentage is worth it.
author: Sipho Nkosi
body:
Easybet has temporarily boosted its welcome bonus to a 150% deposit match for March, up from the standard 100%. It is a limited-time play to attract new sign-ups during what is traditionally a busy month for SA sports betting.

The numbers

Deposit R1,000 and get R1,500 in bonus funds on top. The total playable balance of R2,500 gives you more runway to explore the platform. Maximum bonus amount and qualifying deposit range have not changed from the standard offer.

Wagering requirements remain at 5x on minimum odds of 1.80. That is unchanged from the 100% offer and still fair by SA standards.

Is 150% actually better?

Mathematically, yes - you get 50% more bonus funds on the same deposit. But more bonus means more wagering to clear, even at the same multiple. If you deposit R2,000, you will need to turn over R15,000 in qualifying bets to unlock the R3,000 bonus. That is achievable in 30 days, but it requires consistent betting.

Who should grab this

If you were already planning to join Easybet, the timing is perfect. If you are a casual bettor who places a few bets a week, the standard 100% might actually be easier to clear. The 150% suits active bettors who will naturally turn over the required amount.

Use the Easybet promo code at sign-up and read our Easybet review for the full picture.

title: Hollywoodbets Secures Multi-Year PSL Sponsorship Extension
excerpt: Hollywoodbets extends its PSL sponsorship in a multi-year deal that cements it as SA football betting's most visible brand.
author: Thabo Mokoena
body:
Hollywoodbets has extended its title sponsorship of the Premier Soccer League in a multi-year deal reported to be worth hundreds of millions of rands. The Hollywoodbets Super League and Diski Challenge will continue carrying the brand for at least three more seasons.

Why this deal is massive

The PSL is South Africa's most-watched sports property. Kaizer Chiefs, Orlando Pirates, Mamelodi Sundowns - these clubs command millions of fans who watch every week. Having your brand on the league title is the single most powerful piece of sports advertising you can buy in this country.

Hollywoodbets first took title sponsorship in 2020 and has since become virtually synonymous with SA football betting. The brand recognition this generates is worth far more than any Google ad campaign.

What it means for the market

The sponsorship gives Hollywoodbets a competitive moat that smaller operators simply cannot match. When millions of fans see "Hollywoodbets Super League" every matchday, that is top-of-mind awareness that Betway, Supabets, and World Sports Betting have to work much harder (and spend more) to compete with.

Betway has responded with its own football sponsorships at club level, but league-wide title rights are in a different category entirely.

For punters

Expect more PSL-linked promotions, enhanced odds on Hollywoodbets Super League fixtures, and free-to-play prediction games tied to match days. These promos have historically offered good value, so keep an eye on them during the season.

Read our Hollywoodbets review for the full platform assessment.

title: Ozow Rolls Out Instant Withdrawals at 12 SA Bookmakers
excerpt: Ozow is expanding across SA betting operators, making instant EFT withdrawals the new standard. Here is who supports it and how fast payouts really are.
author: Naledi Khumalo
body:
Ozow is quickly becoming the default withdrawal method for South African betting sites. The instant EFT service now operates across a growing number of licensed operators, and it is changing what punters expect from payout speeds.

How Ozow works

Ozow connects directly to your bank's internet banking system to process instant EFT payments. No card details, no third-party wallets. You authenticate through your bank, and the money moves. For withdrawals, the process is reversed - funds land in your bank account within minutes instead of the 1-3 business days that traditional EFT requires.

Who supports it

Major operators including Betway, Hollywoodbets, Easybet, and Mzansibet now offer Ozow for both deposits and withdrawals. Compatibility covers FNB, Standard Bank, Absa, Nedbank, and Capitec - the big five that account for the vast majority of SA banking customers.

Real-world payout times

In our testing across multiple operators, Ozow withdrawals typically clear in 5-20 minutes once the request is approved. The approval step is where delays can occur - some operators auto-approve repeat withdrawals, while others manually review each request during business hours.

The bigger picture

Instant payouts are becoming a baseline expectation rather than a premium feature. Operators that still take 48 hours to process withdrawals are going to lose customers to those offering Ozow. If fast cashouts matter to you, prioritise operators that support it.

See our full payment methods comparison for a breakdown by operator, or read about Ozow specifically.

title: 10Bet South Africa Raises Welcome Bonus to Record R3,000
excerpt: 10Bet SA raises its welcome bonus to a record level for the SA market. We break down the terms and compare it to the competition.
author: Sipho Nkosi
body:
10Bet has bumped its welcome bonus to what it is calling its biggest-ever offer for South African players. The new deal sits at the top end of the market, but the real question is whether the terms make it worth claiming.

The offer

New registrations get a boosted deposit match with higher caps than 10Bet's standard welcome bonus. The exact percentage and ceiling vary by promotional period, so check the 10Bet promo code page for the current live terms.

How the wagering stacks up

10Bet's wagering requirements have historically been moderate - lower than some competitors but with tighter odds restrictions. The key is the minimum odds qualifier. If the minimum is 2.00, your options for clearing the bonus are more limited than a 1.50 minimum would be.

Compare this with Easybet's 5x at 1.80+ (generous), Betway's tiered structure (varies by promotion), and Hollywoodbets's more straightforward but smaller bonuses.

Should you jump on it?

Record bonuses often come with promotional urgency - limited time, limited availability. Do not let FOMO drive your decision. Read the T&Cs, check the wagering, and only deposit what you would have bet anyway.

Our 10Bet review has the full breakdown on the platform, odds quality, and withdrawal speeds.

title: Western Cape Gambling Board Announces Stricter Advertising Rules for 2026
excerpt: The Western Cape Gambling and Racing Board introduces stricter advertising and player protection rules for licensed operators in the province.
author: Thabo Mokoena
body:
The Western Cape Gambling and Racing Board (WCGRB) has introduced tighter regulations covering advertising practices, deposit limits, and player verification for operators licensed in the province. The changes take effect later this year.

What is changing

The new rules hit three areas:

  • Advertising: Operators can no longer use language that implies betting is risk-free or guaranteed to generate income. Terms like "free money" and "guaranteed wins" in promotional materials are now explicitly prohibited.
  • Deposit limits: Licensed operators must offer mandatory deposit limit tools that are visible during the registration process, not buried in account settings.
  • Verification: Stricter timelines for FICA verification, with operators required to complete checks within 72 hours of the first withdrawal request.

Who does it affect?

Any operator licensed by the WCGRB. This covers several major brands operating in the province. Operators licensed by other provincial boards (Gauteng, KZN) are not directly affected but may adopt similar standards voluntarily, as the trend across provinces is towards stricter oversight.

Good news or bad news?

Good news for punters. Clearer advertising means less misleading marketing. Faster verification means quicker access to your winnings. Deposit limits at registration mean better protection before bad habits form.

The industry will grumble about compliance costs, but these are sensible, player-first changes.

For more on how regulation works in SA, read our FICA verification guide and our responsible gambling resource page.

title: Gbets Launches Early Rugby World Cup 2027 Futures Markets
excerpt: Gbets launches enhanced odds and specials for rugby internationals, including Springbok match markets and try scorer props.
author: Thabo Mokoena
body:
Gbets has rolled out a suite of rugby specials ahead of the international window, with enhanced odds on Springbok fixtures, try scorer props, and multi-bet boosters on rugby markets.

What is on offer

The promotions cover Test matches and selected URC fixtures. Headline features include:

  • Enhanced odds: Boosted prices on Springbok match outcomes, typically 10-20% above standard market odds
  • Try scorer specials: Anytime and first try scorer markets with reduced juice
  • Multi-bet boosters: Bonus percentage added to accumulator winnings when you combine 3+ rugby selections

Are enhanced odds worth it?

Enhanced odds are genuine value when the boost is meaningful and the limits are reasonable. If Gbets offers the Boks at 1.80 when the market is 1.65, that is a real edge. But watch for maximum stake limits on enhanced odds - they are often capped to manage the operator's exposure.

How Gbets compares on rugby

Gbets has been building its rugby credentials steadily (see their Sharks sponsorship). Their rugby market depth is now competitive with the bigger operators. Hollywoodbets and Betway offer more markets per match, but Gbets' promotional pricing can offset that for major fixtures.

Read our full Gbets review for the complete assessment, or grab the Gbets promo code.

title: Supabets Releases Major Mobile App Update with Live Streaming
excerpt: Supabets pushes a major app update with a redesigned interface, quicker bet placement, and improved live betting stability.
author: Naledi Khumalo
body:
Supabets has shipped a significant update to its mobile app, tackling the interface complaints that have dogged it for months. The new version is faster, cleaner, and notably more stable during live betting.

What has improved

The main changes:

  • Navigation redesign: The bottom tab bar now gives quicker access to live events, popular sports, and your open bets
  • Faster bet placement: The lag between tapping "Place Bet" and confirmation has been cut significantly
  • Live betting stability: Odds updates are smoother, with fewer "odds changed" rejections during in-play betting
  • Smaller app size: The download footprint has been reduced, which matters for users with limited storage

Is it enough?

The previous version had real problems. Slow loads, crashes during live events, and a cluttered interface that made finding markets harder than it should be. This update addresses the worst of those issues.

It still does not match Betway's app for polish, and Hollywoodbets's app remains faster on lower-end devices. But the gap has closed. If you uninstalled Supabets out of frustration, it might be time to give it another go.

Where to get it

Android users can download the APK from Supabets' website. iOS users can get it from the App Store. Make sure you are running the latest version to get the full benefit of the update.

Full details in our Supabets review.

title: Blu Voucher Expands to 15,000 Retail Points Across South Africa
excerpt: Blu Voucher expands its retail footprint to over 100,000 outlets in SA, making cash deposits at betting sites easier than ever.
author: Naledi Khumalo
body:
Blu Voucher has expanded its retail network to over 100,000 outlets across South Africa, including Shoprite, Checkers, PEP, and thousands of spaza shops. For bettors who prefer cash deposits, the coverage is now hard to beat.

Why this matters for bettors

A huge number of SA bettors do not have credit cards or prefer not to link their bank accounts to betting sites. Blu Voucher lets you walk into a shop, buy a voucher with cash, and top up your betting account with the voucher code. No bank details, no digital trail.

The expansion to spaza shops is particularly significant. In townships and rural areas where banks and formal retailers are sparse, spaza shops are the primary point of commerce. This brings betting deposits to the doorstep of millions of South Africans who were previously underserved.

Which operators accept Blu Voucher?

Most major SA betting sites now accept Blu Voucher, including Hollywoodbets, Betway, Supabets, Easybet, and Gbets. Check individual operator pages for current support - the list keeps growing.

Any limitations?

Blu Voucher is deposit-only. You cannot withdraw to a voucher - you will still need a bank account for cashouts. Voucher amounts are also fixed (R10, R20, R50, R100, R200) so you cannot deposit an exact custom amount.

Read more about Blu Voucher and other deposit options in our payment methods guide, or see the full Blu Voucher page.

title: Mzansibet Launches R100,000 Lucky Numbers Jackpot Promotion
excerpt: Mzansibet runs a Lucky Numbers promotion with boosted payouts on selected draws. We look at how it works and what the catch is.
author: Sipho Nkosi
body:
Mzansibet is running a promotion on its Lucky Numbers product, offering boosted payouts on selected SA, UK, and international lottery draws. If you already play Lucky Numbers, the enhanced returns might make it worth timing your bets.

How Lucky Numbers works

Lucky Numbers lets you bet on the outcomes of real lottery draws without buying an actual ticket. You pick numbers and bet on whether they will appear in the draw. Payouts are based on fixed odds rather than the lottery prize pool, which means you know exactly what you stand to win before the draw happens.

What the promotion offers

Mzansibet is boosting payouts on selected draws throughout the promotional period. The enhanced odds apply to specific bet types (single numbers, pairs, and combos) on featured draws. Not every draw qualifies, so check the promotions page for the current schedule.

Is it good value?

Lucky Numbers has a higher house edge than sports betting - typically 15-25% depending on the bet type. Boosted payouts reduce that edge temporarily, but it is still a negative-expectation game. Think of it as entertainment with a chance of winning, not as a strategy for making money.

That said, Lucky Numbers is wildly popular in SA. The daily draw schedule means there is always something to bet on, and the results come in fast. If you enjoy it, the promotion is a nice sweetener.

Read our full Mzansibet review for the platform overview, or compare Lucky Numbers offerings across operators.
